Description: Unique identifier for each agent (vehicle, pedestrian, etc.)
X-location (in meters) after transforming origin to bottom-left corner
Y-location (in meters) after transforming origin to bottom-left corner
X-component of speed (in m/s); can be negative
Y-component of speed (in m/s); can be negative
X-component of acceleration (in m/s˛); can be negative
Y-component of acceleration (in m/s˛); can be negative
Lane or segment polygon ID where the agent is located (mapped from image zones)

Most common agent type for the trajectory.
0: Persons (Pedestrians)
1: Bicycles
2: Scooters
3: Passenger Cars (Non-automated)
4: Automated Vehicles
5: Motorcycles
6: Buses
7: Trucks
Smoothed lateral width of the agent (in meters); usually derived from bounding boxes
Smoothed longitudinal length of the agent (in meters); typically includes smoothing over time
